sentences.text: Hey, good morning!
Morning, how are you?
Doing real well, how are you guys?
Doing well.
You have a good weekend?
It was a good weekend, yeah.
How about you?
Yeah, it was great.
I couldn't believe it was kind of like the last weekend of summer, but….
Yes.
It's been flying by, it's a good week.
You guys are… that's a nice conference room.
Oh, yeah.
I'm squinting, I can, I hear Mark's voice, it looks like Mark stands out in that room.
Yes.
Hey, Jane.
Hey, Kyle.
Okay.
We brought on… I don't think you guys had the chance to meet, Megan, who's our VP of Product.
Good morning.
Nice to meet you all.
One….

Well, again, the purpose of the call was to… obviously, you guys mentioned piloting Heliday's Query Builder, and you had a few features on there that you liked, and we were… just about to launch our new Explore Premium the last time we talked, so, we have, like, some key features in there with some market comparisons that made us think that it could be a good fit for what you guys were trying to do, so we'll show you that, and then also just get a sense, of the key deliverables with HelloData right now that you guys like, that we could match up with, and then… kind of go from there.
Is that… unless you guys had anything on your end?
That sounds great.
Well, I'm excited to share with you what we have.

This is a new, a major release that we're about to launch, so I'll be sharing with you our beta system here, and we're gonna go through the Market Insights report within our new Explore feature, and our Market Comparison tool.

So these reports will give you visibility into overall market performance and details about any market or sub-market, even down to a granular custom radius or custom polygon, and you'll be able to compare market performance across a number of key metrics and export that data directly.
So, without further ado… I'll pull that up here, and happy to, answer any questions that you have along the way.
So I'm in ApartmentIQ.
I've got my market survey experience up here, so view my properties and my research properties.
But now I'm in the Explore module, and here I can search on a particular market and look up a specific neighborhood, MSA, anything in between, zip code, or I can go to our market comparison.
So I'll start there just to jump right into it.
I had some filters applied here.

When I go to the market of comparison, it'll automatically show me, just to give a sense of the top 50 MSAs, and I can… I have the ability to compare and view MSAs, counties, and cities, and from here, I can select different metrics.
We've got information about property performance, inventory, rent growth, and as I select, we also have sales transaction data.
And demand through, our demographics information.
So any.
income, income growth, I'll add those to my… my display, and each of these metrics will get added to my table with a single click.
Now, this table, however you choose to configure it, is also exportable directly into an Excel.
So I'll show you a couple more things, and then a snapshot of how quick that Excel generates, that you can use that data as you wish.
But just to go through a little bit more of what's available here.
within each market, if I see something interesting, I assume, if it's fair to say, would you be interested in markets that you're currently operating and new markets nationwide, is my understanding?
Is that correct?
You have, like, a short list of markets that you're currently tracking?
I think all major U.
S.
markets.
Okay, and on an MSA level, city, sub-market, do you… what's kind of your perspective on what level you're looking at at the general tracking?
you know, across the board, I would say, everything from MSA, for some metrics and some, you know, property level for micro-market from the bottom up as well.
Great, okay.
So, our export here that I'll demo today will be the….
Where'd be transaction data from?
We are… we have a partnership with RCA.
Torresa, okay.
MSCI, yeah.
Do you currently work with them, or have you explored their data previously?
Yep.
Oh, you currently have a partnership with them.
We subscribe to the RCA.
Oh, okay, great, wonderful.
So you'll have that data directly embedded into the application, so you'll be able to see that aggregated into the market level.
Do you use… do you use their data feed or their interface, just out of curiosity, for how you're kind of working with the sales transaction data?
I think the broader team, primarily the interface, but our team uses the feed.
Got it, so you have both.
Wonderful.
So you would, again, through the interface here, you'll have the market data, or their sales transaction data, directly in the interface.
And would you have their, you know, RCA transaction ID and property ID in the… in your platform?
We do, yep.
There, there is that, an additional capability that, we can give you the access to those IDs as well.
Great.
I assume you wouldn't need the details, just the IDs?
Well, that way we can link it, yeah, then we can get the details, yep.
Cool.
Yep, that's, all data we store, yep.
Okay, so within, markets, I know, Dallas is of interest, so I can see, again, a snapshot of metrics here across performance, inventory, demand, but I can also, in one click.
zoom in to all of the properties that are currently in that market.
So we're loading a pretty massive MSA.
Took just a couple seconds here.
I can see, I have some subject properties.
Those are those purple, pins that are jumping out to me in my, demo environment here, but I can also see the under construction or scheduled construction that's planned in the next 12 months.
Over here on the left, have you seen this interface, or the part on the left?
I won't spend too much time if you've seen it previously.
I know.
I think it would make sense.
Okay.
Okay, great.
So, you'll have a snapshot of rent and performance, including your rent growth.
We're also, currently, building out the rent forecasts for, 150 MSAs, so those are scheduled to release, this quarter.
We're gonna be rolling those out market by market, so we'll be rolling that out in batches of markets.
And, if I… click in, I can see further details, of the… rent and performance, including historicals, so… Let me go back here, and then, …
I also have the ability to see a property list of all of the properties that are in my view here.
… I'm gonna take a look and just show you another way to work with this, boot.
Phoenix?
Let's say I'm now… maybe I'm interested in Phoenix, I'll go to, … I have, again, in just a couple clicks, I can go to a totally different market.
And I can, review the properties in this area.
Each one of these pins and the list of properties is filterable, so I have different ways that I can search.
Maybe I'm targeting an area in the north of Phoenix.
Again, I have a high degree of customization.
I can drop a radius.
Maybe I'll pull 5 miles in the north of Phoenix.
And all of my data will update and show me the latest information about the rent and performance.
So this will show me the last 30 days rent, rent per square foot, and current concessions.
And I can filter and apply, to a number of different filters, just give you a snapshot here.
Ownership, you have, management company.

That ownership data is something that we're also partnered with RCA on, so that will be coming from RCA, property class, we're also releasing building type, so you'll have a build-to-rent student affordable that will be coming here as well.
Is there any particular, type or.
I need your own property class.
How do we define it?
It's primarily based on rent and vintage, but we look at market and building levels to come up with a definition of property class.
So it's, … What proprietary formula did you have?
Correct.
Got it.
Do you have any documentation on that, by any chance?
We… sure, we can definitely send some, details over as a follow-up.
Excellent.
How… which, source… I know every vendor has a slightly different formula.
What is your current source, and how, how do you feel about that reliability of that data?
You know, right now, we are, from a multifamily perspective in research at least, you know, Kyle's team is using this platform as well, but, we're primarily relying upon RealPage.
… My understanding is their, class is purely based on rent, so top 20% are Class A, bottom 20% are Class CD, and 60 in the middle are Class B, I think, and then, obviously, A+.
Pluses and minuses in there as well.
But I think that's…
a big part of, what we're trying to accomplish with the exercise is some of the, as we look to potentially move off RealPage or supplement, RealPage, like, how are these different?
So I think, you know, the linking to RCA is fantastic, understanding the class difference.

And, you know, We're going through an exercise right now, … in various stages, with RealPage, with HelloData, … Trying to understand, the difference between reported… … You know, surveyed vacancy, and, you know, scraped.
availability measures, and I think we are not making great strides with either vendor, would be a safe thing to say in that, capacity.
You know, I think we're just not… we're seeing very, very different trends where we have overlap.
at the property level between, RealPage's old survey methodology and, the HelloData, methodology.
I don't believe that RealPage has done any overlap in terms of those two.
I think they just pulled… they switched, from a survey-based, as a result of the lawsuit, to a, you know, scraped, inferred, availability, or vacancy, or whatever you want to call it.
And so, given that a lot of in research, our historical.
models are using vacancy.

We'd like to have a longer history of something same-store in the Both the trends and the levels look extremely different at the property level between the Those two methodologies that we have not yet been able to… Explain that.
And secondly, I would just say the RealPage
While the methodology has stated to be changed, the… numbers.
don't reflect that since the switch was made, so I'm just trying to understand.
… How we make… how we think about executing that transition.
Can I ask, Mark, is it… I have an assumption, but is the hello data vacancy lower than you're seeing from the survey vacancy?
Correct.
Okay.
And part of that is attributable.

I mean, obviously the biggest gaps are properties that were in lease-up over the two-year period where we have the… because my understanding from HelloData is that, The apartments will not put out all available units, and so during the lease-up, it's never like a 100% vacancy, slightly moving up, it's more a managed percentage of the units that are shown as available, and so that's a known issue, that I don't have a, …
It's a known issue.
I don't know how we figure out how to, …
or integrate that into our analysis from a, …
Move-forward perspective, if we were to move to a scrape methodology.
Now, RealPage is still reporting, it the way they used to, so, again, using.
Using what they're saying they're doing, versus what's being reported.

I don't know how to close that gap currently, so that's what we're doing a lot of research into, so that's a known issue and something that, you know, we do a fair amount of development, so it's a very valuable piece of information to have, is knowing how long it takes for these properties to lease up, and how many units they're leasing a month.
And so that's one… gap, and then even on the stabilized properties, I would say, yes, there is, … I think systematically we're seeing Hello Data report lower vacancy rates.
For local availability, or whatever the metric is.
Right.

That's what I assumed, and I think maybe, maybe lease-ups are in part, responsible, but I do think, you know, Hello Data historically has largely relied on ILS data, even though, you know, regardless of what they say, but, you know, we scrape over a million property websites every single month.
That's where we find the availability to be the most accurate.
Availability is significantly restricted within… the ILS network at a site like Apartments.
com, so I guess that's the biggest contributor to the gap that you're seeing with their data.
I know we'll have some follow-ups on our side coming out of today, and I… I think we'd be much closer to what you'd expect from, the survey, metrics, so….
Yeah, that'd be great to see.
Thank you.
Got it That's… that's.
occupancy is one of the metrics that we've been tuning and honing over the last 4 years since we started scraping data.
We know that's the most important metric to operators, and And so we've been fine-tuning our, predictions and our algorithms around how we detect if we know, units are being hidden off a website.
Again, it's… it is… happens mostly on lease-ups, where units might not hit the public domain.

But even with underperforming properties, we've got means for detection, in that regard, and for, we've been working with our customers over the years to really enhance and tune our occupancy, so whenever we've, we've done, QA or comparisons, we, almost… we've never, you know, lost a, an accuracy test in comparison, put it that way.
Alright, great.
Okay, so just sharing a couple more of those filters.
I'll just give you a sense of performance-wise, how quick you can zoom in to a particular vintage.
You have any year-built, total units that are top of mind for you in terms of, kind of, asset type, or all across the board?
I think probably larger sizes on average.
Alright, we'll go with 200.
Any preferred vintage?
Check out the 2015 Plus movie.
Alright, so we'll take a look, and… … Narrowed it down pretty quickly for us in this 5-mile radius, and we have those properties, shown here.
I can, again, also click into One of the property pages, and it'll show me the photos.
As available, from us.
we've actually pulled these from our ILS, our rentable, site, so we've got quite, great coverage on our photos.
And then you'll see that, RCA data, shown here.
Plus key building information, and then you'll see those historicals.
So these, data points will reflect our rent, some top-line numbers on rent.
You can also drop into rent per square foot and effective rent.
just out of curiosity, how… how are you looking, and how important are the concessions data to you?
Do you, you know, evaluate market rent on a net effective?
You're looking at gross?
What's your kind of… I know every… Every team looks at the rent metrics slightly differently.
What's your preferred?
Yeah, on the research side, our preferred is definitely not effective.
Cool.
So, we can drop into that net effective.
You can see the gap from subject.
We automatically generate a comp set, so you can see pricing and availability of the subject, the estimated rent roll, and then if I drop in here, I can even see the recommended comps.
In a quick snapshot, but I also have the ability to create and edit a comp report with one click.

So, once I pull that up, it'll use the market survey report to generate a comp report for me, and then from there, I can edit Add, remove, maybe there's a property here that doesn't… quite look like a comp that I'm, or I know the market a little bit better, I want to hone it down, to a set, a narrower set, so you can certainly, work with that in just a click.
And now I'll see I've got a report generated.
Here's that transaction history, again, coming from RCA, plus the new supply.
So, nothing within a 3-mile radius, but maybe I zoom out to 10, and I can really, see that I've got a little bit more new supply coming in, to the… to the heat of my subject.
And remind me, I think this property showed as student.
You do have breakouts for student, and do you have senior housing on the platform?
We do, yep.
So, our, … We do have bill to rent, sued senior and student, yep.
We could isolate all those pretty easily, those types of problems.
That's right, yep.
… And finally, once I get to the bottom of the page, I can see the demographics within a 1, 3, and 5 mile radius, just a key snap… a little bit of a snapshot.
There's some additional demographics on the market level that I'll be able to share here in a moment.

One of the nice things about the platform that, we really make sure that we take into account is that we know you're looking at a lot of inventory, a lot of different properties, so maybe there's something here that catches your eye.
You can quickly navigate and pop that open.
So everything here is meant to be easy to use, quick to navigate.
can quickly see the rent trends.
Again, we have those historicals here, so that you can go back and, and see additional, performance for a number of assets, and see how that asset compares to its comps, and the market at a glance.
Great.
Let's see here… Any other questions before I, continue, or, anything else, Corey or Austin?
I mean, I know we wanted to switch over to see the market summary, export was one of the last things I wanted to make sure I showed.
So, at this point, I've got my export.
I mean, I can take that down into an Excel, I can take it into a CSV.
We're also… Let me stop my screen share, and I'll pull up.
The report, so all of those market data points.
Just…
popped open for me.
So, this is the, again, just in a quick… got each of my MSAs.
state, rent, and all of the selected metrics that I pulled into my view.
Great.
Okay?
Any… Corey, Austin, anything else you wanted to hit on, or questions?
I don't think so.

Chen Chao, I think in the last discussion, as you mentioned the query builder, it felt like it would be relevant to show you what we're about to launch on the market comparison dashboard with some of the additional data points, so… … thoughts or feedback based on what Megan shared?
No, I think it's great.
I think our primary… in the research team specifically, our primary use case is going to be, or ideally, accessing property-level data feeds, and using those for our analytics.
But I think to the extent that we, replace

you know, I know the asset management team's already using, you guys, and to the extent that we replace that more broadly throughout the organization, you know, I think that those are very, very useful, views and data assimilations, so that's… looks… Very user-friendly, very easy to, kind of get the analytics at the top of your fingertips, so that's great.
Yep.
But I think for us, you know, I think the, you know, we've done a… pretty comprehensive comparison.
You know, I think both, are… you got very excited by what's available, from your platform and the Hello Data platform initially.
I think we're… as we move off, some of the traditional players, potentially, that last piece is really, you know.
understanding what the differences are in the methodologies.

It sounds like, you're going to have something exciting, to tell us on the occupancy side, which we look forward to, because I think that is, you know, the rent side, you know, the coverage is fantastic, the rent data, you know, makes sense.
I thought, you know, it's great.

So it's really about how can we Ingest all the information in the easiest fashion for our team, and How can we, you know, think about that gap, or think about how we report lease update and occupancy report?
Can I assume, then, desired next step still remains just some, historical data files so you can continue your inferred against physical occupancy?

Yeah, I think to the extent that we can get actual property-level historical data for a certain market or whatever, just enough of a sampling of properties that we can make some comparisons during the lease-up period and upon stabilization, just to really understand What's driving… the ideal would be, hey, here's how we could get to adjust our historical vacancy, to give it something similar to what's being reported today, in a systematic fashion, and… but, you know, also on a go-forward basis is even more important.
How do we understand the lease-up of a property, in terms of leasing velocity, and then how do we understand, you know.
What's happening out there in the markets today?
Okay?

And, I think the request, the last time we spoke was as far back as possible, ideally 4 or 5 years, but… and obviously, we've started with, with, DFW, and I believe New York… New York City, but if I'm hearing you, Mark, maybe, the two markets we've already… worked with you on would be sufficient, maybe even one, and then as much data as we're willing to share historically.
I guess I was curious if, you know, if 2 years back would be sufficient, or how far back you'd really need for your test.
I mean, we can have 3.
Yeah, two or three would be great.
If we're gonna do, like, a DFW is fine.
I don't think we… New York is a little sparse for RealPage anyway, so…
DSL, you'd be great.
Again, Austin, any questions related to that request?
I think we understand what the team's trying to accomplish and what they need, but….
Maybe we'll apply that 200 unit count filter?
Does that work?
Will it give you, sort of, the largest properties in the market?
It's a good question.
I mean, we do have plenty of 50-plus unit properties.

The more important thing is to get enough of… just make sure we have enough of sample size as we're making the comparison so that we get enough lease-up properties to make that comparison get enough stabilizers to… So, we can work with whatever you're able to share, but….
Great.
The more that comparison, the more the better.
We'll, we'll share the minimum unit count.
The larger the properties, I think you'll, you'll find a good test result.
Sounds great.
Sounds good.
Okay, great.
That's all I had.
Okay, we'll be in touch with the timeline on our side.
Thanks for jumping on today, and then we'll circle back shortly thereafter to see what you found and answer questions.
Sounds great, sounds good.
Thank you.
Appreciate it.
Yes, thank you.
